examples.title: Single Agent
examples.description: One agent with bash and file tools, followed by streaming and multi-turn conversation examples.

examples.title: Team Collaboration
examples.description: Use runTeam() to turn a goal into a coordinated multi-agent task graph and final result.

examples.title: Task Pipeline
examples.description: Use runTasks() with an explicit task DAG, assignments, and dependency-scoped context.

examples.title: Multi-Model Team
examples.description: Combine models from different providers with custom tools in one agent pool.

examples.title: Adaptive Customer Support
examples.description: Let runTeam() select the specialists needed for a shipping or billing escalation and synthesize a grounded response.

examples.title: Contract Review DAG
examples.description: Run a four-task contract review DAG with parallel checks and step-level retry.

examples.title: Incident Postmortem DAG
examples.description: Fan three independent incident investigations into root-cause analysis and a final postmortem.

examples.title: Meeting Summarizer
examples.description: Process a transcript in parallel into a summary, structured action items, and sentiment.

examples.title: Competitive Monitoring
examples.description: Monitor multiple sources in parallel, detect contradictions, and aggregate an intelligence report.

examples.title: Paper Replication Triage
examples.description: Reconcile multiple evidence sources into a structured paper-replication go or no-go plan.

examples.title: Rare Disease Information Triage
examples.description: Audit isolated mock evidence sources, detect conflicts, and apply an explicit medical safety boundary.

examples.title: Personalized Interview Simulator
examples.description: Run an interactive interviewer and observer loop with shared memory and a structured debrief.

examples.title: Narrative Puzzle Hint Arbitration
examples.description: Resolve conflicting hint sources through parallel analysis, synthesis, and an external safety veto.

examples.title: Translation and Backtranslation
examples.description: Translate with one provider, backtranslate with another, and flag semantic drift with structured output.

examples.title: Fan-Out and Aggregate
examples.description: Send the same question to multiple agents in parallel and aggregate their results.

examples.title: Agent Handoff
examples.description: Delegate a synchronous sub-prompt from one specialist to another with delegate_to_agent.

examples.title: Multi-Perspective Code Review
examples.description: Generate code, run several specialist reviews in parallel, and synthesize structured findings.

examples.title: Consensus
examples.description: Use a proposer and a panel of judges in a configurable refutation and quorum loop.

examples.title: Research Aggregation
examples.description: Run independent research tasks in parallel and collate them through a synthesis task.

examples.title: Event-Driven DAG
examples.description: Use deferred promises to show a downstream task starting when its dependency is satisfied without waiting for an unrelated task.

examples.title: Cross-Provider Reasoning
examples.description: Preserve a reasoning model's thought stream as text when a conversation moves between providers.

examples.title: Structured Output
examples.description: Validate an agent's JSON response against a Zod output schema.

examples.title: Task Retry
examples.description: Retry failed tasks with configurable delay and exponential backoff.

examples.title: Risk-Gated Bash
examples.description: Combine tool grants with a per-call gate that allows, reviews, or blocks bash commands.

examples.title: Plan Replay
examples.description: Freeze a coordinator plan into a diffable artifact and replay it without another planning call.

examples.title: Offline Evaluation Regression
examples.description: Run a no-key EvalSet regression across two targets with rule and judge scorers plus a gate.

examples.title: Observability v2
examples.description: No-key examples for batching, trace stores, OpenTelemetry, lifecycle handling, and the offline Run Viewer.

examples.title: Cost-Tiered Pipeline
examples.description: Compare the token and cost profile of an all-flagship pipeline with a tiered model mix.

examples.title: Online Evaluation Sampling
examples.description: Sample live runs for asynchronous scoring in a durable FileEvalStore with explicit shutdown.

examples.title: Trace Observability
examples.description: Collect structured onTrace events for LLM calls, tools, tasks, and agent runs.

examples.title: Express Customer Support
examples.description: Embed a structured runTasks() support pipeline behind an Express POST /tickets endpoint.

examples.title: MCP GitHub
examples.description: Connect an MCP server over stdio and expose its tools through the OMA tool registry.

examples.title: Vercel AI SDK
examples.description: Combine OMA runTeam() orchestration with an AI SDK streaming chat interface in Next.js.

examples.title: External Agent over ACP
examples.description: Use an external coding CLI over ACP as a team member in an OMA task DAG.

examples.title: TencentDB Agent Memory
examples.description: Back an OMA team's shared memory with TencentDB-Agent-Memory through its Hermes Gateway.

examples.title: External Agent Process
examples.description: Run deterministic local subprocesses as external agents without a model API key.

examples.title: MCP Bilig WorkPaper
examples.description: Use Bilig WorkPaper MCP tools for formula readback, recalculation, and persisted workbook data.

examples.title: MCP Open Design
examples.description: Drive multiple asynchronous Open Design MCP jobs in parallel through an explicit task pipeline.

examples.title: Engram Memory
examples.description: Use Engram for manual agent memory tools or as the shared-memory store behind runTeam().

examples.title: Azure OpenAI
examples.description: Run the standard three-agent team with Azure OpenAI deployments.

examples.title: AWS Bedrock
examples.description: Run the standard three-agent team through the AWS Bedrock Converse API.

examples.description: Run the standard three-agent team through GitHub Copilot's OpenAI-compatible endpoint.

examples.description: Run the standard three-agent team through ByteDance Volcengine Ark.

examples.title: Google Gemini
examples.description: Run the standard three-agent team with the native Google Gemini adapter.

examples.title: Gemma 4 Local
examples.description: Run both execution modes locally with Gemma 4 through Ollama and no cloud API key.

examples.title: Grok
examples.description: Run the standard three-agent team with xAI Grok models.

examples.title: Groq

examples.title: Tencent Hunyuan
examples.description: Run the standard three-agent team with Tencent Hunyuan through MaaS.

examples.description: Tune sampling and tool-call behavior for a quantized local model on vLLM or llama-server.

examples.description: Run the standard three-agent team with MiniMax models.

examples.description: Mix a local Ollama model with a cloud model in one task pipeline.

examples.description: Run the standard three-agent team through OpenRouter's OpenAI-compatible API.
